old_claim.note: Leather Tanning provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The scoped shelter components are manufactured leather panels, so conversion of animal hides into durable leather is a constitutive material process.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract reports ZooMS analysis of eight leather panels from one Roman military tent dated to Vindolanda's first occupation.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The scoped tent evidence consists specifically of manufactured cattle- and sheep-hide leather panels from one shelter.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Without durable leather there is no artifact matching this node's panel scope, so the material process is required rather than merely helpful.

ontology_before: Leather was treated as one optional route to a broad hypothetical prehistoric tent frame.
ontology_after: Leather is constitutive of the directly evidenced Roman tent-panel artifact.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: hide_tent_frames retains the leather_tanning edge but strengthens it from enabling to required under the Roman panel scope.
would_reject_if: The Vindolanda panels were reidentified as untreated hides or as objects unrelated to a military tent.
short_reason: Leather is the defining material of the surviving Roman tent panels.
